readings.title: Roofline: An Insightful Visual Performance Model for Multicore Architectures
readings.authors: Samuel Williams, Andrew Waterman, David Patterson
readings.note: The foundational 2009 paper introducing the Roofline model. Provides the framework for classifying kernels as compute-bound or memory-bound based on arithmetic intensity versus machine balance. Essential reading for performance analysis.

readings.title: RISC-V Privileged Specification v1.12 (Virtual Memory Section)
readings.note: The authoritative specification for RISC-V Sv39 and Sv48 virtual memory. Defines the page table entry format, page walk algorithm, and superpage support. Reference for the Sv39 content in Lecture 2.

readings.title: Computer Organization and Design: RISC-V Edition, Chapter 5 (Large and Fast: Exploiting Memory Hierarchy)
readings.authors: David A. Patterson and John L. Hennessy
readings.note: The standard textbook treatment of caches, virtual memory, and TLBs. Chapter 5 covers direct-mapped through set-associative caches, the three C's, write policies, and multi-level hierarchies with worked examples.

readings.title: Chips and Cheese: AMD's Zen 4 Part 2 -- Memory Subsystem and Conclusion
readings.note: Detailed reverse-engineering analysis of AMD Zen 4's cache hierarchy and TLB structure. Includes measured latency data for L1, L2, L3, and V-Cache configurations. Source for the real cache specifications cited in Lecture 1.

readings.title: A Deep Dive into Intel's Alder Lake Microarchitectures
readings.note: Comprehensive analysis of Intel Golden Cove microarchitecture including cache hierarchy, TLB sizes, and pipeline details. Source for Intel cache and TLB specifications used in both lectures.

readings.title: AMD's 7950X3D: Zen 4 Gets V-Cache
readings.note: Analysis of AMD's 3D V-Cache technology that bonds a 64 MB SRAM chiplet on top of the CCD using TSMC SoIC stacking. Discusses the latency penalty and hit rate improvements of the expanded L3 cache.

readings.title: Apple Silicon Memory and Internal Storage
readings.note: Overview of Apple M-series memory architecture including the System Level Cache (SLC), exclusive caching policy, and unified memory design. Explains how Apple's approach differs from x86 architectures.

readings.title: MIT 6.S081: Virtual Memory Lecture Notes
readings.note: Clear, concise lecture notes on virtual memory from MIT's operating systems course. Covers page tables, TLBs, and the RISC-V Sv39 translation algorithm with excellent diagrams.

readings.title: NERSC Roofline Model Documentation
readings.authors: NERSC (National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center)
readings.note: Practical guide to using the Roofline model for performance analysis on real HPC systems. Includes instructions for measuring arithmetic intensity and constructing hierarchical Roofline models.
